scenario: A Java Job Worker built with Spring Zeebe handles payment-processing Service Tasks. The worker is annotated with @JobWorker(type = "payment-processing"). Under heavy load, the team observes the worker times out activated jobs (jobs return to the broker, re-activate, repeat). Which Spring Zeebe @JobWorker parameter primarily controls how long the worker has before the job times out?
question: Which Spring Zeebe @JobWorker parameter primarily controls how long the worker has before the job times out?

options.text: maxRetries -- controls how many times the worker retries a job on failure before Zeebe creates an Incident. This parameter addresses what happens after a job fails (retry count), not how long the worker has to process a currently-active job. Configuring maxRetries in response to timeout-induced re-activations will not extend the activation window; jobs will still expire and re-activate, incrementing retry counts until an Incident fires.

pollInterval -- configures how frequently (in milliseconds) the worker polls the Zeebe broker for new jobs to activate in each polling cycle. This parameter controls acquisition frequency, not processing duration. A shorter pollInterval means the worker checks for jobs more often; it has no effect on how long the worker has to complete a job it has already activated. Reducing poll interval will not prevent timeout-induced re-activations.

timeout -- sets the job-activation timeout duration in the @JobWorker annotation, specifying how long Zeebe waits for the worker to complete, fail, or otherwise handle an activated job before re-activating it for another worker instance. The typical default is 5 minutes; if payment processing takes 8 minutes but the timeout is 5, jobs expire and re-activate repeatedly. Increase timeout to a value exceeding the maximum expected processing duration to eliminate the re-activation loop.

requestTimeout -- sets the maximum wait time for individual gRPC request-response operations at the transport layer, such as job activation requests and completion acknowledgements. This controls network-level call timeouts, not the duration allocated for job processing logic. Increasing requestTimeout will not extend the time window between job activation and required completion; it only affects how long the client waits for individual gRPC responses.

a.text: Incorrect. @JobWorker(type="...", timeout=...) sets the job-activation timeout -- how long Zeebe waits for the worker to handle this job before re-activating it. If your payment processing takes 8 minutes but default is 5, jobs time out and re-activate, causing the symptom observed.

b.text: Incorrect. maxRetries handles failure retry, not timeout.

d.text: Incorrect. requestTimeout handles gRPC-level timeouts, not job processing.
